title: NoSQL Data Modeling Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about best practices for data modeling in NoSQL databases.

questions.question: What's the most important rule of NoSQL data modeling?
options: Model for Objects first and then queries.
Model for the Queries your application will perform, not just the data themselves.

questions.question: When should you generally prefer 'Normalization' (Referencing) over 'Denormalization' (Embedding)?
options: When data is updated frequently or shared across many different documents.
When you want to avoid using any IDs.
When the data is very small.

questions.question: Which of these is a typical benefit of using a 'Computed Field' in a NoSQL document?
options: It saves read-time CPU by performing a common calculation once on the write and storing the result.

questions.question: In a document database, you should generally have ____ collections compared to a SQL database.
What's the primary reason to use UUIDs or Snowflake IDs rather than auto-incrementing integers as primary keys in NoSQL?
options: They are smaller than standard integers.
They can be generated independently on multiple servers without a central 'counter', which is essential for distributed scaling.
To automatically generate a backup of the database.
